title: Life on Mars? Organic Material Found
description: NAU planetary scientist Mark Salvatore explains NASA's Curiosity findings of organic molecules and methane in Martian rocks. He discusses what those results suggest about past habitability and how to read them when searching for signs of life.
impact: Radiant advances planetary remote sensing by connecting NAU researchers to active Mars exploration and interpretation. Work like Salvatore's helps translate rover data into a deeper understanding of planetary surfaces, past habitability, and the search for meaningful chemical signatures beyond Earth.
